design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate procedurally hazardous levels, manage limited special attacks (Megacrush bombs), acquire weapon upgrades, defeat waves of enemies and environmental obstacles to progress toward final objective
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (health depletion, limited bomb uses) versus sustained forward momentum through enemy-dense levels; tension between exploration for pickups and direct engagement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite mercenary operative conducting covert military operations; piloting stolen vehicles; commanding overwhelming firepower against overwhelming odds
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Run-and-gun design philosophy emphasizing constant forward pressure and enemy aggression over defensive positioning. Arcade-era design that respects player skill through weapon variety and Megacrush resource management rather than difficulty gates. The Genesis version adds economy layer (weapon shops, character switching) that introduces strategic choice absent from arcade original.

difficulty.lives_system: Health gauge that gradually depletes on damage; death occurs when vitality reaches zero. First-Aid Kits and power-ups restore partial or maximum vitality respectively.
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; no mid-stage checkpoints documented. Six main levels plus final rescue stage.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through weapon selection (Assault Rifle, Shotgun, Grenade Launcher, Flamethrower) and Megacrush bomb deployment strategy. Genesis version adds character switching and weapon shop purchases for customization. Player must manage consumable resources (bombs, health items) discovered during levels.

progression.upgrade_system: Arcade: Weapon pickup system allowing four gun variants (Assault Rifle → Shotgun/Grenade Launcher/Flamethrower). Megacrush bombs as special attack resource. Genesis Original Mode: Currency-based weapon shop system where defeated enemies drop money for purchasing upgrades and character-specific loadouts.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level completion unlocks subsequent stages. Genesis Original Mode introduces weapon shop access throughout stages for mid-game customization.
replayability_hooks: Arcade Mode vs Original Mode in Genesis version providing different plots and stages
Multiple playable characters in Genesis Original Mode with distinct weapon loadouts
Vehicle piloting sections offering mechanical variety within levels
High score chase and speedrun potential via skill-based weapon management

world_building_techniques: Fictional nation setting (Zutula with apartheid government) establishes geopolitical context
Character naming and assignment (Joseph Gibson/Howard Powell/Thomas Clarke as 'Wolf Force' team) creates squad identity
Rescue objective (former President from rebels) frames narrative stakes
Genesis Original Mode introduces alternative plot with new stages, suggesting narrative variety

danger_signals: Enemy soldier deployment density escalates per stage
Vehicle combat sections suggesting military infrastructure
Final stage Hercules Transport assault indicating fortified objective
environmental_storytelling: Level progression through fictional African nation suggests military occupation
Vehicle piloting opportunities imply captured enemy infrastructure available for player use
Final stage setting (transport plane) establishes extraction/escape narrative tension
